tier_intro: What do you actually sell? Your AI agent needs to know your offering inside out — so it can answer questions accurately, handle objections, and never promise something you don't deliver. File uploads welcome here.

tier_complete.headline: Product knowledge loaded.
tier_complete.body: Your AI agent now knows what you sell, how you price it, what makes it special, and what it can't do. It will never over-promise or give a customer false expectations. Next: teach it who your customers are.

questions.question: Describe your main products or services — or upload a catalog, menu, or product list.
questions.text_placeholder: Or describe your main offerings in your own words. Even a rough list helps.
questions.meta_profiling: How detailed and organized the product description is reveals brand maturity. Single offerings vs. complex catalog = very different AI needs.
questions.why_this_matters: This is the core knowledge your AI agent needs. Without it, every product question gets deflected. With it, customers get accurate answers instantly.

questions.question: What are your top 3 most popular or most important offerings?
questions.placeholder: Name and one-line description for each. These are what AI should know best.
questions.meta_profiling: Top sellers reveal where AI effort should concentrate. If they match the full catalog upload, no issue. If different, signals a priority mismatch to investigate.
questions.why_this_matters: AI prioritizes depth of knowledge on your most important offerings — so the 80% of questions about your top products get the best answers.

questions.question: How do you charge for your products or services?

options.label: Fixed price — customers know the cost upfront
Hourly rate — time-based billing
Subscription — recurring monthly or annual fee
Quote-based — every project is different, we give custom prices
Freemium — free tier plus paid upgrades
Tiered plans — basic / pro / enterprise (or similar)
Marketplace / commission — we take a percentage
Mixed — different pricing models for different offerings

questions.meta_profiling: Pricing model complexity determines how much the AI can answer vs. deflect. Quote-based = AI can never give prices, only route to sales.
questions.why_this_matters: AI must know whether it can quote prices or not. Getting this wrong creates either false commitments or frustrating non-answers.

questions.question: How open is your pricing?

options.label: Fully public — prices are on our website, AI can share them
On request — we share pricing when asked, but it's not public
Quote only — AI should never give a specific number, always route to sales
Negotiable — we have list prices but deals happen, AI should be careful

questions.meta_profiling: Pricing transparency is a compliance decision. Quote-only or negotiable means AI needs strict guardrails to avoid under- or over-committing.
questions.why_this_matters: Nothing breaks trust faster than an AI quoting a price that the sales team then overrides. This setting prevents that exact scenario.

questions.question: What makes you genuinely different from competitors? Why do customers choose you?
questions.placeholder: Be honest. What do you actually do better? Speed? Quality? Price? Service? Niche expertise?
questions.meta_profiling: Quality and specificity of USP reveals how clearly the company understands its market position. Vague answers = positioning work needed.
questions.why_this_matters: When a customer compares you to a competitor, AI needs a genuine, compelling answer. This is it. Weak here = lost sales.

questions.question: Do you offer any guarantees, warranties, or promises to customers?
questions.placeholder: e.g. '30-day money back', '2-year warranty', 'satisfaction guaranteed or we redo it', 'SLA 99.9% uptime'
questions.meta_profiling: Presence and specificity of guarantees signals customer confidence and brand maturity. Absence may indicate a missed retention opportunity.
questions.why_this_matters: Guarantees reduce purchase hesitation. AI proactively mentions them when customers are on the fence — but only if it knows they exist.

questions.question: What should AI tell customers you CAN'T help with, or things you DON'T offer?
questions.placeholder: e.g. 'We don't do rush orders', 'We only serve B2B', 'No refunds on digital downloads', 'No international shipping'
questions.meta_profiling: Limitations reveal operational boundaries and common misunderstandings. Detailed answer = company has dealt with many out-of-scope requests.
questions.why_this_matters: AI that clearly communicates 'that's outside our scope' and redirects appropriately saves everyone time. Better than a frustrated customer who expected something different.

questions.question: Does your offering change seasonally or by time of year?

options.label: No — our offering is consistent year-round
Yes — we add or remove products seasonally
Yes — pricing or demand varies by season
Campaign-based — we run sales and promos at specific times
Heavily seasonal — our whole business shifts by season

questions.meta_profiling: Seasonality signals whether AI knowledge base needs scheduled updates and whether it should flag when information might be outdated.
questions.why_this_matters: An AI describing last season's menu or discontinued products creates real problems. Flagging seasonality triggers update reminders.

questions.question: Do you do custom or bespoke work?

options.label: Standard offerings only — no customization
Some customization — we can adapt within limits
Fully custom — every project is unique, scope varies
Custom work available at additional cost

questions.meta_profiling: Custom work = higher conversation complexity. AI needs to know when to gather requirements vs. quote directly.
questions.why_this_matters: Custom work requests need different handling — more questions, different pricing process. AI behaves differently when it knows this is a custom inquiry.

questions.question: How do you deliver what you sell?

options.label: Physical delivery / shipping
In-store or in-person pickup
On-site service (we come to the customer)
Digital download / instant access
SaaS / online platform access
Meetings / consultations / calls
Combination of the above

questions.meta_profiling: Delivery method affects the types of questions customers will ask. Physical = tracking, shipping times. Digital = access issues, passwords. Service = scheduling.
questions.why_this_matters: Delivery type shapes 30-40% of all customer inquiries. AI needs to know whether to help with tracking, scheduling, access credentials, or something else entirely.

questions.question: What is your return, refund, or cancellation policy? (upload or describe)
questions.text_placeholder: One sentence to a paragraph is enough. Or upload the policy doc.
questions.meta_profiling: Presence and clarity of refund policy signals customer trust investment. Vague or missing policy = customer service friction.
questions.why_this_matters: Refund and return questions are among the most common. AI that can answer them confidently and accurately reduces support tickets and customer frustration.

questions.question: Any other product or service information you want AI to know about?
questions.placeholder: Awards, certifications, partnerships, accreditations, production methods, supply chain highlights — anything that builds credibility
questions.meta_profiling: Additional context reveals what the company is proud of but often forgets to mention. These details strengthen AI responses when trust-building matters.
questions.why_this_matters: The things companies are proudest of often aren't in the FAQ. This is where those trust-builders live — and AI uses them at the right moment.
